In their view and creed, rulings pertain only to what lies in the heart, not to outward actions. And the more pretentious among them insist that l ilha ill Allh does not encompass all aspects of life. The result of this corrupt ideology has been the spread of wickedness across the earth, the suspension of jihd in the path of Allah, and the rise of shirk, biddah, and political, economic, intellectual, and social deviations among the Muslims, whereby the true meanings of the Sharah have been lost.

"NEW: The confession of the perpetrator of the Tadamon Massacre, Amjad Yousesf:

 The individual appearing in the viral footage known as the Tadamon massacre is me, Amjad Youssef. These events date back to the early years of the Syrian revolution. 

 At the time, I was accompanied by Najib al-Halabi and a photographer whose name I cannot recall. We rounded up 40 individuals at random, under the pretext that they were 'terrorists'.

 We took them to a pre-dug pit, where I began executing them, as shown in the video. In some instances, I waited for the victim to fall into the pit before firing; in others, I shot them before they fell. 

 Intermittently, I would call on Najib al-Halabi to take his turn in the killing, until we had executed everyone present.

 We had previously placed car tires at the bottom of the pit. After the executions, we piled more tires over the bodies and set them on fire to prevent the scent of decomposition from spreading throughout the area. We then filled the pit. 

 I received no military orders to carry out this massacre, it was my own personal decision. 

 I cannot explain why there were exactly 40 victims, the selection was arbitrary, dictated solely by the circumstances at the time.

Source: Usman A Khan and translation by Syrian journalist Qusay Noor"

"Sayyidun Abdullh ibn Umar    was among the most devoted, scrupulous, and steadfast Companions of the Messenger of Allh . He was a man whose life became a living embodiment of the Sunnah, so precise in his adherence that he would follow even the smallest actions of the Prophet  out of love and reverence.

He was Abdullh ibn Umar ibn al-Khab al-Adaw al-Qurash, the son of the great Caliph Sayyidun Umar ibn al-Khab   . He was born in Makkah before the Hijrah and embraced Islm at a young age alongside his noble father. He emigrated to al-Madnah al-Munawwarah at the age of ten, and thus was counted among the early Muhjirn, those whom Allh, Most High, praised in His Book:

And the foremost, the first of the Muhjirn and the Anr and those who followed them with excellence, Allh is pleased with them, and they are pleased with Him, and He has prepared for them gardens beneath which rivers flow, wherein they will abide forever. That is the great success.

[Qurn 9:100]

In his youth, he longed to participate in battle alongside the Messenger of Allh . He was presented at Badr but was turned back due to his young age. He was again presented at Uud, when he was only thirteen years old, hoping to join the ranks, but he was sent back by the Prophet  due to his youth, alongside Sayyidun Usmah ibn Zayd   . That moment weighed heavily upon him, and he later said: 

A night like that never came my way, filled with sleeplessness, sadness, and weeping, because the Messenger of Allh  did not accept me.

After returning him from Uud, the Messenger of Allh  appointed him among those tasked with guarding the children in Madnah. 

Yet his heart remained attached to striving in the path of Allh, and his sincere supplication to be permitted to fight was soon answered. In the fifth year after Hijrah, when he reached the age of fifteen, he was allowed to participate in the Battle of al-Khandaq. 

From that point forward, he took part in the major campaigns of Islm. He participated in the campaign against Ban Qurayah, the journey to al-udaybiyyah, and was among those who pledged beneath the tree in the Pledge of al-Riwn, a moment honored by Allh, Most High:

Indeed, Allh was pleased with the believers when they pledged allegiance to you under the tree. He knew what was in their hearts, so He sent down tranquility upon them and rewarded them with a near victory.

[Qurn 48:18]

He later joined the campaigns of Khaybar, Mutah, the Conquest of Makkah, the expedition against Ban Judhaymah, the Battle of unayn, and the march to Tabk. He was also among the soldiers of the army of Sayyidun Usmah ibn Zayd   . 

After returning from this expedition, he volunteered in the wars against apostasy and had the honor of participating in the Battle of Yammah against Musaylamah al-Kadhdhb. 

He later marched to Shm and fought at al-Yarmk, then joined the campaigns in the Persian lands under Sayyidun Sad ibn Ab Waqq   , attending al-Qdisiyyah, Jall, and the conquest of al-Madin. He also took part in the conquest of Egypt and campaigns in North Africa. 

Even in his later years, he joined the expedition toward Constantinople, about which the Prophet  said: The first army to attack the city of Caesar is forgiven.

What distinguished Ibn Umar    above all was his extraordinary commitment to following the Sunnah. He would carefully observe where the Prophet  prayed, where he sat, and even the paths he walked, then imitate them with remarkable precision. 

If he saw the Prophet  perform prayer in a particular place, he would later pray in that very same place. If he saw him make supplication while standing, he would supplicate while standing; and if he saw him invoke Allh while sitting, he would do the same. His life was not merely one of narration, but of complete imitation.

He was among the foremost scholars of the Companions and a prolific narrator of hadith, transmitting over 2,600 narrations. His knowledge was paired with deep caution, he was known for his reluctance to issue legal verdicts without certainty, and he preferred silence over speculation. 

Due to his age, knowledge, and unwavering adherence to the Sunnah, he became known in his time as the Shaykh of the abah, and even powerful governors such as al-ajjj ibn Ysuf treated him with respect and deference due to his immense status amongst the Muslims. From the greatest testimonies to his precision in transmitting the Sunnah is that he stands at the head of one of the most authentic chains in hadith, the Golden Chain.

Mlik  Nfi  Ibn Umar.

A generation of leading Tbin took knowledge from him, including Sad ibn al-Musayyib, Thbit al-Bunn, ws, al-Zuhr, Muammad ibn Srn, Nfi (his freed servant), Mujhid, and many others. His influence thus extended deeply into the next generation of scholarship. 

His legacy was also carried forward through his own household, as several of his sons became scholars, most notably Slim ibn Abdullh ibn Umar, who was counted among the great jurists of Madnah. Through both his students and his family, his knowledge and methodology continued to shape the transmission of the Sunnah for generations.

During the periods of civil strife, having lived to witness both the First and Second Fitnah among the Muslims, Ibn Umar    consistently adopted a principled stance of restraint. He presented himself at the sittings of arbitration, demonstrating his desire for unity among the Muslims. 

He famously said: When you call me to prayer, I respond. However, when you call me to fight a Muslim, I do not. He never raised his sword against the believers. Yet, on the other hand, he answered every call to strive against the disbelievers, embodying the words of Allh, Most High:

Humble toward the believers, powerful against the disbelievers; they strive in the cause of Allh 

[Qurn 5:54]

He was known for his humility, generosity, and detachment from worldly life. Wealth would come to him, yet he would spend it freely in charity. He freed many slaves and cared deeply for the poor and needy. His nights were often spent in prayer, and his days in fasting and remembrance.

Sayyidun Abdullh ibn Umar    passed away in Makkah in 73 AH and was buried near Dh uw, leaving behind a legacy of unwavering adherence to the Sunnah and a life defined by sincerity and caution.

May Allh be pleased with Sayyidun Abdullh ibn Umar   , the devoted follower of the Sunnah and one of the greatest scholars among the Companions of the Messenger of Allh ."

""Pakistan hosted the U.S.-Iran negotiations in Islamabad for one overriding reason: survival.

A day before the talks began, Saudi Arabia and Qatar announced $5 billion in financial assistance to Pakistan. The same day, Saudi Arabias defence ministry confirmed that Pakistani fighter jets had landed at King Abdulaziz Air Base under the mutual defence pact signed in September 2025.

These two important details reveal the transactional nature of the Pakistan-Saudi-Qatar relations that put Pakistan in an precarious position. 

Pakistan shares a 900km border with Iran. It has watched Iranian ballistic missiles and drones strike Saudi and GCC targets for weeks. Its economy is in intensive care, dependent on IMF lifelines and Gulf deposits to keep foreign reserves above collapse thresholds. It cannot afford a war with Iran. Militarily, Pakistan has the hardware. Financially, it would be ruined.

The 2025 defence pact with Saudi Arabia states that aggression against one is aggression against both. That pact now obliges Pakistan to act. Saudi Arabia has been struck almost daily by Iran for more than a month now. 

Mediation was Pakistans escape route. A successful deal in Islamabad would have ended the war and neutralised the pacts trigger. No war means no obligation to get involved and no Iranian missiles aimed at Pakistani soil. Pakistan gets to keep the $5 billion, stay out of the conflict, and maintain relations with Tehran.

But the talks have failed. Vance left Islamabad without a deal after 21 hours of negotiations. The ceasefire is fragile and Strait of Hormuz remains contested.

Now Pakistan faces the worst of all outcomes. It has taken the money. Its jets are on Saudi soil. The defence pact is active. And the war is not over.

$5 billion does not cover the cost of a war with Iran. It barely covers Pakistans debt repayment to the UAE this month. It is a down payment on compliance, not compensation for the devastation that would follow.

Pakistan must now make a choice: honour the pact and enter a war it cannot sustain, or refuse and break with Riyadh. There is no middle ground left."

"France: Palestinian children, who fled Gaza Genocide, separated from parents and placed under state care

A Palestinian family evacuated from Gaza to France say French authorities have separated three children from their parents after allegations of abuse that the family strongly denies and courts have not substantiated with evidence. 

The abduction took place in cooperation with an organisation that describes itself as supporting Gaza genocide survivors.

Three siblings, Rabhi (11), Nour (10), and Hussam al-Din (8), arrived in France with their father and grandfather after fleeing Israels genocide in Gaza. The French Foreign Ministry facilitated their evacuation in late 2023. Their mother, Raghda Al-Sheikh, remained trapped in Gaza, hoping to reunite with them later.

According to the childrens grandfather, Rabhi Al-Sheikh, the incident began in Grenoble, where the family had settled after evacuation. Women who presented themselves as close family friends regularly spent time with the children and gained the familys trust over several months.

In July 2024, they took the children under the pretext of enrolling them in a summer camp. The children did not return.

He later discovered that the women were volunteers with a Paris-based Palestinian association known as Palestine Nous Rassemble. He said they had never disclosed this affiliation before the incident.

Shortly after taking the children, the women filed a complaint accusing the father of abuse.

No medical or school evidence presented. The family firmly denies the allegations. They say no medical or school reports support claims of abuse. 

According to testimony presented in court, even the judge acknowledged the absence of documented evidence.

During a hearing on March 30, 2026, the children reportedly expressed a desire to reconnect with their family. Lawyers representing the father, the children, and social services all supported restoring contact between the children and their parents.

A reception center in Moristelle also recommended rebuilding family ties.
Despite this, the judge extended the childrens placement in state care for another year, relying primarily on the associations account, according to the family.

Meanwhile, the childrens mother remains in Gaza, where she has been displaced and now lives in a shelter after her home was destroyed.

She does not know who is caring for her children or how they are being raised. She also fears they may lose their language, religion, and cultural identity after nearly two years of separation.

Source: Quds News Network"
